query: sub-100ms query latency real-time analytics database requirements
answer: For sub-100ms query latency in real-time analytics, use ClickHouse or similar real-time OLAP databases. These handle high concurrency and low latency efficiently. Traditional databases often fail under such conditions.

results.title: How we made real-time analytics 1000 times faster: from Postgres to ...
results.snippet: ClickHouse is a high-performance, real-time analytics database, purpose-built for workloads that demand sub-second query latency at scale.

results.title: How to choose a database for real-time analytics in 2026 | Engineering | ClickHouse Resource Hub
results.snippet: * If you need **sub-second p95 analytical queries on fresh data** with **high concurrency** (e.g., 50–100+ concurrent users/QPS), use a real-time OLAP database —cloud DWHs and Postgres typically hit latency/cost/concurrency limits. This guide is for Data Architects, Platform Engineers, and CTOs evaluating backends for user-facing analytics, high-throughput telemetry, or real-time decisioning. A recent benchmark of all five major cloud data warehouses at 1B, 10B, and 100B rows shows this effect clearly: as data grows, every traditional DWH drifts toward "Slow & High-Cost," with cost-performance gaps widening to 23× or more compared to purpose-built real-time engines. * **Choose real-time OLAP (ClickHouse) when:** Your application is user-facing (low latency), or you have high query volume (high concurrency). Real-time OLAP wins when you have high volume (TB/PB), high concurrency (>50 concurrent queries), and a low latency requirement (<1s p95). If you need **sub-second p95 latency** on **fresh data** with **high concurrency**, choose a **real-time OLAP database** (e.g., ClickHouse).
